key_insight: Laser welding depth strongly affects lap-joint strength, and a calibrated FEM workflow can estimate joint quality from the welding parameters.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a practical route to predict and tune laser welding settings for improving the load-bearing performance of stainless-steel lap joints.
method: Built lap-joint specimens from X5CrNi18-10 steel plates.
Performed laser welding with both partial and full penetration.
Combined mechanical testing, macroscopic inspection, and Abaqus FEM simulations to validate the numerical model.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental tests on two welding depths and compares simulation results against measured strength and macroscopic observations.
watch_item: The model is validated on a specific material, geometry, and two penetration cases, so broader process transferability may require additional calibration.
paper_title: Analysis of the Influence of Laser Welding Parameters on the Quality of the Lap Joint
